step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("quercetin biapigenin nanoparticles blood-brain barrier penetration study")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

(No need to repeat prior discussions.)
After reviewing the newly retrieved evidence, I note that although several general principles about nanoparticle delivery and BBB penetration are discussed—including successful use of various nanocarriers (like polymers, lipids, micelles)—there is no mention of *quercetin-biapigenin* nanoparticles specifically. Furthermore, while quercetin alone is noted as having vasoprotective and capillary-stabilizing properties, none of the results confirm its ability—or that of its combination with biapigenin—to cross the blood-brain barrier via nanoparticle delivery. Given the absence of targeted studies on this exact formulation, the claim of effectiveness in BBB penetration lacks empirical validation.
